contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 20
- Description: DTC/B2B audience labels + PNW corridor Hit List sweep (141 new venues)
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/90
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/91
  
  Details:
  ## What changed
  
  Two discrete pieces shipped in one turn — both in `market_research`.
  
  ### 1. Audience labels — DTC on newsletters, B2B on retailer outreach
  
  Added top-level Gmail audience labels parallel to the existing per-campaign / per-stage labels so the operator can filter "all consumer-facing email I've ever sent" or "all retailer outreach" with a single Gmail click, across every campaign, ever:
  
  | Pipeline | Existing label | New parallel label |
  |---|---|---|
  | `send_newsletter.py` | `Newsletter/<campaign>` | **`DTC`** |
  | `suggest_warmup_prospect_drafts.py` | `AI/Warm-up` (→ `AI/Sent Warm-up`) | **`B2B`** |
  | `suggest_manager_followup_drafts.py` | `AI/Follow-up` (→ `AI/Sent Follow-up`) | **`B2B`** |
  
  Both labels applied in a single `messages.modify` call so each thread carries them atomically. The `AI/*` lifecycle swap downstream (in `sync_email_agent_followup.py`) leaves the `B2B` label alone — only the `AI/<X>` ↔ `AI/Sent <X>` pair gets touched. Considered nesting (`DTC/Newsletter/<campaign>`) but rejected — would rename every existing thread for marginal taxonomy gain. Parallel is purely additive; existing saved Gmail searches keep working unchanged.
  
  ### 2. PNW corridor sweep — Portland → Seattle → Spokane → Boise
  
  New `pnw_corridor` region preset on `discover_apothecaries_la_hit_list.py` covering three connected highway segments in one preset:
  
  - **I-5 PDX → SEA** — Portland OR, Vancouver WA, Longview/Kelso, Centralia/Chehalis, Olympia, Tacoma, Seattle.
  - **I-90 SEA → SPK** — Bellevue/Eastside, North Bend/Snoqualmie, Cle Elum, Ellensburg, Moses Lake, Spokane.
  - **US-95 SPK → BOI** — Coeur d'Alene, Moscow ID, Lewiston ID, McCall ID, Boise ID.
  
  18 centers, 50 km overlapping disks, `allowed_states={OR, WA, ID}` to gate Place Details against MT/CA/NV bleed. Bounding box generous enough for the Idaho panhandle.
  
  **Live run on the PR appended 141 new venues to Hit List** (411 raw Places returned across 18 centers, 141 unique after dedup against the existing 667 store keys / 496 place_ids / 511 name+geo fingerprints / 523 name+address keys — strong signal that production sweeps had not yet touched I-90 east of Seattle or US-95 south through Idaho).
  
  The 141 new rows land with `Status=Research` and get picked up by the existing GitHub-Actions promotion pipeline (`hit_list_status_promote` → `hit_list_research_photo_review` → `hit_list_enrich_contact`). They'll move through the canonical `AI:*` ladder (`AI: Shortlisted` → `AI: Photo needs review` → `AI: Enrich with contact` → `AI: Email found` / `AI: Contact Form found` → `AI: Warm up prospect`) — same flow every other region uses, now consistent with the canonical Status values aligned in `dapp#192` earlier this session.
  
  ## Why
  
  Operator framed both:
  
  - "For the DTC emails sent, we should also label them as that in my inbox" → DTC label.
  - "B2B label" → symmetrical B2B label on the warm-up + follow-up flows.
  - "There is a python script that populates Hit List … only extends to Portland Oregon. Could you do a sweep for the areas along highway 5 from Portland to Seattle? Also from Seattle all the way to Spokane and the Boise Idaho." → PNW corridor preset + live sweep.
  
  ## Pull requests
  
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/90 (audience labels)
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/91 (PNW corridor + live sweep)
- Contributor(s): Garyjob <garyjob@gmail.com>
- TDG Issued: 33.33
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
